re_line: Yoon gets 30 years for NK drone infiltration · Military grounds 47 Miron helicopters · NK slams US missile sale approval
morning_memo: Seoul court sentenced former President Yoon to 30 years prison for authorizing drone flights over Pyongyang
Military suspended LAH-1 Miron helicopter operations after discovering engine corrosion in 47 of 57 aircraft
SK Hynix selected Nasdaq over NYSE for planned US listing worth up to $50 billion

key_stat.label: LAH-1 Miron helicopters grounded
key_stat.context: Defense procurement agency found engine corrosion in 47 of 57 helicopters assembled by Hanwha Aerospace, highlighting quality control issues in domestic military production.

bp_locations.name: Yongbyon Nuclear Complex
bp_locations.note: Apr 2026: Enrichment building expansion complete, new centrifuge hall operational. 5 MWe reactor and IRT-2000 both running. Thermal signatures consistent with increased plutonium/HEU production.

bp_locations.name: Sinpo South Shipyard
bp_locations.note: Feb 2025: Hero Kim Kun Ok SLBM submarine exited drydock after 2+ years of fitting-out but still not operational as of May 2025. New submarine construction begun in separate bay.

bp_locations.name: THAAD Site — Seongju County
bp_locations.note: Mar 2026: All 6 THAAD launchers redeployed to Middle East amid Iran tensions. At least 2 launchers may have returned to Seongju by late Mar 2026. Battery operational status uncertain.

bp_locations.name: Sohae Satellite Launch Station
bp_locations.note: May 2026: Rocket engine test conducted last week, possibly for military reconnaissance satellite program revival. New viewing area building nearing completion. Previous reports showed 2,500 kN engine test stand firing and facility expansion.

bp_locations.name: Punggye-ri Nuclear Test Site
bp_locations.note: Nov 2025: ROK DIA assessed Tunnel No. 3 is test-ready and a nuclear test can be conducted at any time upon political decision. Site maintained at high readiness since mid-2024.

bp_locations.name: Tumangang–Khasan (NK-Russia border)
bp_locations.note: Satellite imagery shows new road bridge construction accelerated, completion target moved to Summer 2026. Passenger rail service resumed mid-2025. Ore and tank car traffic at highest levels since project began.

bp_locations.name: Sinuiju–Dandong (NK-China border)
bp_locations.note: Mar 12, 2026: Cross-border passenger train service resumed between Sinuiju and Dandong after COVID-era suspension. New Yalu River Bridge may open in 2026.

bp_locations.name: Rason SEZ
bp_locations.note: Nov 2025: Chinese businesspeople re-entry to Rason SEZ expanded. Coal exports through Rason at highest levels in years throughout 2025. Arms export berth also active.

bp_locations.name: Yellow Sea NLL
bp_locations.note: Sep 26, 2025: 140m DPRK merchant vessel Toksong crossed NLL near Baengnyeong Island, advanced 5km south. ROK Navy fired ~60 warning shots; vessel retreated after 1 hour. Ship switched AIS nationality to China and flew Chinese flag. First NLL intrusion in three years.

bp_locations.name: Yellow Sea PMZ
bp_locations.note: Atlantic Amsterdam (converted oil rig, Chinese aquaculture platform) relocated out of PMZ Jan 27-28, 2026 to Weihai shipyard following Lee Jae-myung Beijing state visit. Two giant aquaculture cages (Shen Lan 1, Shen Lan 2) and 13 buoys remain in the PMZ.

bp_locations.name: Vostochny/Dunai (Russian Far East)
bp_locations.note: Jan 2026: Arms shipments slowed due to ice — one Russian vessel (likely Angara or Lady R) visited Rason, docked Jan 14 at arms export berth. Cargo routed to Vostochny. Since Sep 2023: 64 voyages by 4 vessels delivered ~15,800 containers from Rajin to Vostochny/Dunai.

rok_government.action: Launches investigation into missing ammunition at Daejeon prison
rok_government.detail: Ministry investigating disappearance of 100 rounds of live ammunition at Daejeon correctional facility. Investigation launched recently after discovery of missing rounds from guard armory.

rok_government.action: Suspends LAH-1 Miron helicopter operations over engine defects
rok_government.detail: Military suspended flight operations of locally manufactured LAH-1 Miron helicopters after discovering corrosion in engines of 47 aircraft. Additional cracks found in 38 units, affecting 83% of fleet.

rok_government.action: Says too early to confirm Korea-US summit during G7
rok_government.detail: Senior Seoul official stated it is premature to determine whether Presidents Lee and Trump would hold bilateral summit during upcoming G7 summit in Italy.

calendar_watch.headline: Korean War 76th anniversary
calendar_watch.detail: Commemoration of June 25, 1950 Korean War outbreak. Traditionally marked by presidential speeches and inter-Korean tensions.

calendar_watch.headline: BOK Monetary Policy Board meeting
calendar_watch.detail: Bank of Korea rate decision. Current policy rate at 2.50% following June adjustment.

calendar_watch.headline: Section 122 surcharge expiry deadline
calendar_watch.detail: 150-day statutory limit on Section 122 global tariff surcharge expires. Congress must extend or tariff authority lapses.

calendar_watch.headline: Korean War Armistice 73rd anniversary
calendar_watch.detail: Anniversary of 1953 armistice signing. Often accompanied by DPRK military exercises or provocations.

calendar_watch.headline: Korean Liberation Day
calendar_watch.detail: National holiday marking liberation from Japanese rule. Politically significant presidential address expected.

overnight_items.headline: S. Korea, U.S., Japan hold trilateral talks on North Korea
overnight_items.body_text: South Korea held trilateral consultations with U.S. and Japanese officials on North Korea policy coordination. The meeting addressed Pyongyang's continued weapons development and military cooperation with Russia, while reaffirming commitment to Camp David trilateral framework despite recent bilateral tensions.

overnight_items.headline: North Korea condemns US missile sale approval to South Korea
overnight_items.body_text: North Korea's foreign ministry condemned U.S. approval of missile sales to South Korea through KCNA statement. Pyongyang characterized the arms transfer as destabilizing regional security and warned of corresponding measures to strengthen its own deterrent capabilities against what it termed hostile military buildups.

overnight_items.headline: Election protests enter 9th day over ballot shortages
overnight_items.body_text: Protests demanding a re-run of local elections continued for ninth consecutive day with 700 demonstrators gathering at Seoul's Olympic Handball Gymnasium. The June 3 elections saw unprecedented ballot shortages at 26 polling stations nationwide, preventing some voters from casting ballots in mayoral and gubernatorial races.

top_stories.headline: Former President Yoon sentenced to 30 years for drone infiltration
top_stories.body: Seoul Central District Court sentenced former President Yoon to 30 years in prison for authorizing military drone flights over Pyongyang that scattered propaganda leaflets. The court ruled Yoon abused presidential authority and endangered national security by ordering the missions without cabinet approval. The verdict marks the harshest criminal sentence for a former South Korean president since military dictator Chun Doo-hwan received life imprisonment in 1996.
top_stories.so_what: Verdict sets precedent for prosecuting security policy decisions by former presidents

top_stories.headline: SK Hynix to list on Nasdaq in up to $50B offering
top_stories.body: SK Hynix selected Nasdaq over NYSE for its planned U.S. secondary listing that could value the memory chip maker at up to $50 billion, according to sources familiar with the matter. The world's second-largest memory chip producer aims to complete the listing by year-end, capitalizing on surging demand for high-bandwidth memory chips used in AI applications. The listing would mark the largest Korean tech IPO in the U.S. market and provide SK Hynix with dollar funding for capacity expansion.
top_stories.so_what: Listing provides SK Hynix direct access to U.S. capital markets amid semiconductor competition

top_stories.headline: Military grounds entire Miron helicopter fleet over engine defects
top_stories.body: South Korea's military suspended all LAH-1 Miron helicopter operations after discovering engine corrosion in 47 of 57 aircraft assembled by Hanwha Aerospace. Defense procurement officials found cracks in diffuser components of 38 helicopters' engines, originally developed by French firm Safran. The grounding affects Korea's indigenous light armed helicopter program worth 1.2 trillion won ($790 million), raising questions about quality control in domestic defense manufacturing.
top_stories.so_what: Fleet grounding disrupts Army aviation operations and delays indigenous helicopter program
top_stories.pattern_note: Similar quality issues plagued K2 tank power pack development, delaying deployment by years

also_today.headline: Ex-election chief banned from leaving country amid ballot probe
also_today.body_text: Justice ministry approved travel ban for former National Election Commission chairman amid investigation into June 3 local election ballot shortages that disrupted voting at 26 polling stations nationwide.

also_today.headline: US tightens medical device export controls to North Korea
also_today.body_text: Treasury Department published list requiring enhanced licensing for medical device exports to North Korea. New controls affect diagnostic equipment and surgical instruments previously allowed under humanitarian exemptions.

also_today.headline: Hwang In-beom leads Korea's 2-1 World Cup comeback over Czech Republic
also_today.body_text: South Korea defeated Czech Republic 2-1 in World Cup opener with second-half goals from Hwang In-beom. Match drew smaller crowd than expected at Guadalajara's Estadio Akron despite Korean community presence.

also_today.headline: Arrest warrants sought for ex-Shincheonji officials
also_today.body_text: Investigators sought arrest warrants for three former Shincheonji religious group officials over suspicious financial ties to opposition People Power Party during previous administration.

investment_package.latest_update: Korea-US Strategic Investment Corporation established following National Assembly approval (Mar 12, 2026)

trade_policy.detail: Launched Mar 11 2026 against 16 trading partners including South Korea; could provide new legal authority for tariffs post-Section 122 expiry (Jul 24 2026)
Samsung and SK Hynix China fabs operating under annual BIS export licenses (renewed Dec 30 2025 for 2026); maintenance/incremental upgrades only, no expansion
Foreign Entity of Concern rules affect Korean EV battery makers sourcing from China; phase-in: battery components Dec 2024, critical minerals Dec 2025
Samsung received preliminary $6.4B CHIPS award (Apr 2024) for Taylor TX fabs; 10-year restriction on materially expanding semiconductor manufacturing in China

tariff_tracker.headline_note: Section 122 global surcharge effective Feb 24 2026, expires Jul 24 2026 unless Congress extends

sector_rates.note: No Korea exemption; all country exemptions eliminated Mar 2025
Reduced from 25% under US-Korea deal, retroactive to Nov 1 2025
Reduced under US-Korea deal per USTR Dec 3 2025 implementation
Effective Jan 15 2026; broader semiconductor tariffs pending Apr 14 2026 report

tariff_tracker.section_122_surcharge: 10% global surcharge (Section 122, effective Feb 24 2026, expires Jul 24 2026)
tariff_tracker.last_change: Feb 24 2026 — Section 122 surcharge imposed following Supreme Court IEEPA ruling
tariff_tracker.next_trigger: Jul 24 2026 — Section 122 surcharge expiry (150-day statutory limit); Apr 14 2026 — Commerce/USTR semiconductor tariff report due

business_economy.headline: Battery stocks lag as China pressure offsets AI demand
business_economy.body_text: Korean battery manufacturers' shares underperformed broader market despite AI-driven energy storage demand. Chinese competitors' aggressive pricing and overcapacity concerns weighed on LG Energy Solution and SK On valuations despite growing data center opportunities.

business_economy.headline: NS Shopping wins approval for Homeplus Express acquisition
business_economy.body_text: Korea's Fair Trade Commission approved NS Shopping's acquisition of Homeplus Express convenience store chain. Deal strengthens NS Shopping's offline retail presence with 300+ stores nationwide as e-commerce competition intensifies.

business_economy.headline: Lee Jung-hoo's 18-game hitting streak ends
business_economy.body_text: San Francisco Giants outfielder Lee Jung-hoo saw his career-best 18-game hitting streak end for the first time in nearly a month. The Korean rookie maintained a .318 batting average through 65 games in his debut MLB season.

northeast_asia.headline: Italy's Meloni and Lee deepen bilateral ties
northeast_asia.body_text: Italian Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni and President Lee Jae Myung agreed to strengthen cooperation on advanced technologies and defense industries. Leaders signed four MOUs covering science cooperation and joint development projects in Africa.

northeast_asia.headline: Chinese expert: Yoon verdict warns against security crimes
northeast_asia.body_text: Chinese academic characterized former President Yoon's 30-year sentence as strong deterrent against actions threatening regional stability. Global Times coverage emphasized verdict as cautionary message for South Korean political leadership on inter-Korean relations.

northeast_asia.headline: Tumen River tests China's North Korea ties
northeast_asia.body_text: Analysis examines how cross-border infrastructure development along Tumen River reflects competing Chinese and Russian influence over North Korea. Joint economic projects remain stalled despite renewed diplomatic engagement between Beijing and Pyongyang.

opeds_today.headline: North Korea's wall-to-wall coverage of Xi visit holds up Kim as global leader
opeds_today.central_argument: North Korean state media used Xi Jinping's visit to elevate Kim Jong Un's international stature by presenting him as an equal partner to China's leader.
opeds_today.summary: Rodong Sinmun published extended edition devoted to Xi's first state visit in seven years. Coverage emphasized Kim's role as global statesman receiving major power leader. Media presentation aimed to bolster domestic legitimacy through international recognition.
opeds_today.policy_so_what: Propaganda framing affects North Korean elite perceptions of regime stability and international leverage.

opeds_today.headline: Has the Japan-DPRK Pyongyang Declaration Been Abandoned?
opeds_today.central_argument: Chongryon's quiet platform revision signals North Korea may be abandoning the 2002 Pyongyang Declaration framework for Japan-DPRK normalization.
opeds_today.summary: Analysis examines implications of pro-Pyongyang association Chongryon removing references to Japan-DPRK normalization from its platform. Change suggests North Korea may have given up on diplomatic normalization with Japan. Development carries significant implications for Tokyo's approach to abduction issue and sanctions policy.
opeds_today.policy_so_what: Platform change affects Japan's diplomatic options for addressing abduction issue and regional security coordination.

key_quotes.speaker: Kim Jong Un
key_quotes.quote: North Korea will offer full support and solidarity to the Russian army and people in carrying out the special military operation in Ukraine
key_quotes.source_article: North Korea's Kim voices full support for Russia, sends National Day message to Putin, KCNA says

kcna_delta.bottom_line: Kim Jong Un reaffirmed North Korea's support for Russia's war in Ukraine through National Day message while maintaining no public appearances for sixth consecutive day.
